disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

questions.questionText: Category 3 water is dangerous because it…
options: May contain pathogens, sewage and toxic contaminants

questions.questionText: Porous materials saturated by Category 3 water are generally…
options: Removed and disposed of, as they cannot be reliably decontaminated

questions.questionText: PPE for Category 3 work typically includes…
options: Respiratory protection, full body/skin protection and eye protection

questions.questionText: Disposal of Category 3 waste in Australia must…
options: Follow the state regulatory and waste requirements
Be washed to the storm drain

questions.questionText: Before restoration can be verified complete you must…
options: Clean, sanitise, dry and document to a defensible standard

questions.questionText: A risk assessment for Cat 3 work is…
options: Essential, and drives the controls and PPE
